# terrible_community_sops_load_vars (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against

### Optional

- `expressions` (String) This option controls how Jinja2 expressions in values in the loaded file are handled. If set to `ignore`, expressions will not be evaluated, but treated as regular strings. If set to `evaluate-on-load`, expressions will be evaluated on execution of this module, in other words, when the file is loaded. If set to `lazy-evaluation`, expressions will be lazily evaluated. This requires ansible-core 2.19 or newer and is the same behavior than `ansible.builtin.include_vars`. `lazy-evaluation` has been added in community.sops 2.2.0.
- `file` (String) The file name from which variables should be loaded. If the path is relative, it will look for the file in `vars/` subdirectory of a role or relative to playbook.
- `name` (String) The name of a variable into which assign the included vars. If omitted (`null`) they will be made top level vars.

### Read-Only

- `ansible_facts` (String) Variables that were included and their values.
- `ansible_included_var_files` (String) A list of files that were successfully included.
